findings.issue: InvitationCompletePage captures organization_slug from acceptInvitation response and tells the user 'Redirecting to ${orgSlug}...' in the success card body (InvitationCompletePage.tsx:111), but the setTimeout actually navigates only to `${appBase}` (the bare base URL) on line 80, completely ignoring the captured orgSlug. The UX is internally contradictory — the user is shown a destination that they will not be sent to.
findings.remediation: Either navigate to `${appBase}/${orgSlug}` (with slash normalization) to match the displayed message, or drop the orgSlug state and the 'Redirecting to ${orgSlug}...' body so the message is accurate.

findings.issue: InvitationPage.handleOAuthLogin interpolates the URL-decoded token from useParams() directly into a returnTo URL path without encodeURIComponent, while the corresponding API calls (getInvitation/acceptInvitation) defensively use encodeURIComponent(token) in api.ts:43,55. A token containing '/', '?', '#', or other reserved characters would either break the URL or produce a returnTo that the OAuth callback cannot safely round-trip back. The 'Invitation accepted!' flow depends on this same returnTo being reconstructable.
findings.remediation: Wrap the token in encodeURIComponent: const returnTo = `${appBase}/invitations/${encodeURIComponent(token)}/complete` — matching the defensive encoding already in use in api.ts.

findings.issue: anySignal adds 'abort' event listeners to every caller-supplied signal but never removes them. The combined controller is returned to makeRequest and the controller closure keeps a strong reference to the listener. The listener is only released when the original signal itself aborts (via the {once:true} option) or when both the listener and the controller are otherwise garbage-collected. In React StrictMode, OAuthCallbackPage and InvitationCompletePage each construct an AbortController per useEffect run and re-run effects, so listener closures from aborted runs can transiently pile up on the timeout signal before the timeout fires. There is no dispose hook for makeRequest callers to call.
findings.remediation: Track every addEventListener registration, and after fetch settles in makeRequest's finally block, removeEventListener on each input signal. Or have anySignal return a dispose function that makeRequest calls unconditionally in finally.

findings.issue: InvitationCompletePage success path discards any 'return_to' query param and unconditionally redirects to the bare appBase after a 2s delay, while OAuthCallbackPage (the equivalent flow for non-invitation sign-in) honors 'return_to' with isSafeReturnTo validation. Users who land on /invitations/<token>/complete?return_to=%2Fprojects%2F42 from an in-app link will be dropped at the dashboard root rather than the page they intended. The qp URLSearchParams object already has the return_to value available — it is simply not read.
findings.remediation: Read qp.get('return_to'), apply the same isSafeReturnTo guard used in AppRouter.tsx:117, and navigate to `${appBase}${safeReturnTo}` (or `${appBase}/${orgSlug}${safeReturnTo}` if you also adopt the orgSlug fix) instead of `${appBase}`.

findings.issue: Multiple OAuth error-path tests do not assert that access_token is absent from localStorage after the failure. The 'oauth callback: handles redeem failure', 'oauth callback: handles oauthError param', 'oauth callback: handles missing handoff_id/app_code', and 'oauth callback: handles missing stored verifier' tests in AppRouter.test.tsx (and the corresponding 'shows error when oauthError param is present' / 'shows error when handoff_id and app_code are missing' tests in InvitationCompletePage.test.tsx) only assert sessionStorage cleanup. If a future change accidentally called localStorage.setItem('access_token', ...) on the error path, the tests would not catch it — the production code is correct today, but the gap means a regression would silently authenticate a user on the error path.
findings.remediation: Add `expect(localStorage.getItem('access_token')).toBeNull()` to each of the error-path tests that mutates the failure path.
